intro: Sundamomum macroglossa, also known as the large-lipped wild ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce pain and swelling, as well as its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
